rationale: In the citing paper, Barasch and Berger (2014) is used mainly as background evidence that self-focused attention can influence word-of-mouth content (e.g., more self-presenting communication). The citation appears with other prior studies to describe an existing stream and motivate the gap on affective decision processing. The paper does not build its hypotheses, methods, or empirical tests on this specific article, and it neither extends nor challenges its core claims directly.
